title: CustomFeatureDependencies Object
description: A collection of dependencies associated with a particular custom feature. These are the entities that the custom feature is dependent on. If these entities are modified, it will cause the custom feature to recompute so it can be up to date. These dependencies are saved with the custom feature and can be accessed at a later time, typically during the compute, to access and use the entities.

methods.name: add
methods.description: Adds an entity or parameter that this feature is dependent on. This is used by Fusion to know when to recompute this feature and to control the behavior of the feature's node in the timeline.

methods.name: classType
methods.description: Static function that all classes support that returns the type of the class as a string. The returned string matches the string returned by the objectType property. For example if you have a reference to an object and you want to check if it's a SketchLine you can use myObject.objectType == fusion.SketchLine.classType().

methods.name: deleteAll
methods.description: Deletes all of the current dependencies. This method is for convenience and is equivalent to iterating through the collection and deleting them one at a time.

methods.name: item
methods.description: Function that returns the specified custom dependency using an index into the collection.

methods.name: itemById
methods.description: Function that returns the specified custom dependency given its ID.

properties.name: count
properties.description: The number of CustomFeatureParameter objects in the collection.

properties.name: isValid
properties.description: Indicates if this object is still valid, i.e. hasn't been deleted or some other action done to invalidate the reference.

properties.name: objectType
properties.description: This property is supported by all objects in the API and returns a string that contains the full name (namespace::objecttype) describing the type of the object.   It's often useful to use this in combination with the classType method to see if an object is a certain type. For example: if obj.objectType == adsk.core.Point3D.classType():

add.title: CustomFeatureDependencies.add Method
add.description: Adds an entity or parameter that this feature is dependent on. This is used by Fusion to know when to recompute this feature and to control the behavior of the feature's node in the timeline.
add.return_description: Returns the created CustomFeatureDependency object and asserts if it failed.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: An ID for this dependency. This is used to allow you to identify which dependency is which in the future. The ID must be unique with respect to the other dependencies of this custom feature.

parameters.name: entity
parameters.description: The entity or parameter you want to add as a dependency. This can be a BRepBody, BRepFace, BrepEdge, BRepVertex, a sketch, any sketch entities, a profile, any construction geometry, or any parameter.

classType.title: CustomFeatureDependencies.classType Method
classType.description: Static function that all classes support that returns the type of the class as a string. The returned string matches the string returned by the objectType property. For example if you have a reference to an object and you want to check if it's a SketchLine you can use myObject.objectType == fusion.SketchLine.classType().
classType.return_description: Returns a string indicating the type of the object.

deleteAll.title: CustomFeatureDependencies.deleteAll Method
deleteAll.description: Deletes all of the current dependencies. This method is for convenience and is equivalent to iterating through the collection and deleting them one at a time.
deleteAll.return_description: Returns true if the operation was successful.

item.title: CustomFeatureDependencies.item Method
item.description: Function that returns the specified custom dependency using an index into the collection.
item.return_description: Returns the specified item or null if an invalid index was specified.

parameters.name: index
parameters.description: The index of the item within the collection to return. The first item in the collection has an index of 0.

itemById.title: CustomFeatureDependencies.itemById Method
itemById.description: Function that returns the specified custom dependency given its ID.
itemById.return_description: Returns the specified item or null if the specified ID was not found.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The ID of the dependency, which was assigned when the dependency was defined.

count.title: CustomFeatureDependencies.count Property
count.description: The number of CustomFeatureParameter objects in the collection.

syntax.python:
# Get the value of the property.
propertyValue = customFeatureDependencies_var.count

isValid.title: CustomFeatureDependencies.isValid Property
isValid.description: Indicates if this object is still valid, i.e. hasn't been deleted or some other action done to invalidate the reference.

syntax.python:
# Get the value of the property.
propertyValue = customFeatureDependencies_var.isValid

objectType.title: CustomFeatureDependencies.objectType Property
objectType.description: This property is supported by all objects in the API and returns a string that contains the full name (namespace::objecttype) describing the type of the object. It's often useful to use this in combination with the classType method to see if an object is a certain type. For example: if obj.objectType == adsk.core.Point3D.classType():

syntax.python:
# Get the value of the property.
propertyValue = customFeatureDependencies_var.objectType
